title: Fix Unexpected Waveform when using always_comb and always_ff
body: Hi, I encountered an unexpected waveform difference when running the same testbench in Verilator and QuestaSim. The design includes both combinational logic (always_comb) and sequential logic (always_ff). ``` always_comb begin data_n = data_q; // Latch previous value out_valid = 0; if (in_valid) begin data_n = in_data; // Update immediately when valid out_valid = 1; end end always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin if (~rst_n) begin data_q <= '0; end else begin data_q <= data_n; // Sequential update end end assign out_data = data_q; assign out_data_prev = data_n; // Expected to reflect previous data_q ``` it seems that Verilator and QuestaSim handle this differently. Verilator Output (GTKWave): QuestaSim Output(Vsim GUI): Expected Behavior: out_data_prev should hold the previous out_data value and not update immediately. The behavior in QuestaSim is expected, while Verilator’s behavior appears incorrect. Possible Issue with Combinational + Sequential Logic - The signal out_data_prev is assigned from data_n, which is updated inside always_comb. - In QuestaSim, data_n correctly holds the previous data_q value until in_valid is asserted. - In Verilator, data_n seems to update in a way that does not match traditional event-driven simulation, likely due to different scheduling in combinational logic. I'd like to try to help with this, but I don't know where to start. So any insights/help would be appreciated!
code_blocks:
always_comb begin
  data_n = data_q;  // Latch previous value
  out_valid = 0;
  if (in_valid) begin
    data_n = in_data; // Update immediately when valid
    out_valid = 1;
  end
end

always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
  if (~rst_n) begin
    data_q <= '0;
  end else begin
    data_q <= data_n; // Sequential update
  end
end

assign out_data = data_q;
assign out_data_prev = data_n; // Expected to reflect previous data_q
